title: erica glauca var. elegans
intro: Erica glauca var. elegans, also known as the elegant bell heather, is a variety of the Erica glauca plant species, belonging to the heath family (Ericaceae). It is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Erica glauca var. elegans has been used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to offer health benefits such as reducing stress, improving respiratory health, and boosting the immune system.
